title: SSE 1 MiB buffer cap with SseFrameTooLarge terminates stream on hostile/buggy servers
description: subscribe_events() caps the in-memory SSE line buffer at 1 MiB and yields ClientError::SseFrameTooLarge if that limit is exceeded. A future contributor might remove this as premature optimization. It is a correctness requirement: a server that sends a stream with no double-newline terminator (buggy or hostile) would otherwise accumulate data unboundedly until OOM. The 1 MiB limit is generous for any legitimate JMAP StateChange event and gives the caller a clean error to log and reconnect on, rather than a silent process death.
close_reason: Decision record — current design is correct, no change needed
